core_mechanism: a lossless boundary-encoding / dimensional-reduction principle in which the complete state of an N-dimensional bulk region is fully reconstructable from data living on its (N-1)-dimensional boundary, with the information capacity bounded by the boundary's area (a fixed number of degrees of freedom per unit area) rather than by the enclosed volume
brief_reason: The abstract states that a three-dimensional world can be reconstructed as an image of data stored on a two-dimensional projection using only one discrete degree of freedom per Planck area, yet rich enough to describe all three-dimensional phenomena, which is an area-bounded bulk-to-boundary encoding.
